scenarios.title: Information Retrieval Toolkits
scenarios.when_to_read: The task asks how to use, install, debug, serve, evaluate, or maintain Pyserini or Anserini-backed Python IR workflows.
scenarios.role: Pyserini-specific routing and operational guidance for sparse Lucene retrieval, dense/Faiss retrieval, evaluation/fusion, REST/MCP serving, and source checkout maintenance.
scenarios.read_when: pyserini, anserini, LuceneSearcher, LuceneIndexer, FaissSearcher, pyserini.encode, pyserini.eval, trec_eval, mcpyserini, Pyserini REST, No matching jar file found, pyjnius, faiss missing
scenarios.best_for: Installing Pyserini, indexing/searching/fetching with Lucene, building dense encoder/Faiss commands, evaluating TREC/MS MARCO/KILT runs, configuring REST/MCP servers, or selecting focused Pyserini source tests.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is generic IR theory, unrelated Lucene Java development, generic FastAPI/MCP usage without Pyserini, or another repository's retrieval stack.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `pyserini` when user language names Pyserini or its public modules/CLIs, when errors involve Pyserini's Java/Faiss/runtime resources, or when an agent needs self-contained recipes for Pyserini workflows without reopening the source checkout. Prefer narrower generic skills only when the user is not using Pyserini.
